QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_43

question.text: A company with post-issue capital of 10 crore shares plans an IPO at Rs. 150 per share. The promoter currently holds 1.5 crore shares. How many additional shares must the promoter acquire to meet the minimum contribution requirement?

answer.text: No additional shares needed; 1.5 crore shares exceed the requirement

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Minimum promoters' contribution = 20% of post-issue capital = 20% of 10 crore = 2 crore shares. The promoter currently holds 1.5 crore shares. Shortfall = 2 crore - 1.5 crore = 0.5 crore shares. The promoter must acquire an additional 0.5 crore shares to meet the 20% threshold.
**The Trap:** "No additional shares needed" is wrong because 1.5 crore out of 10 crore is only 15%, which is below the 20% minimum. Candidates who confuse pre-issue and post-issue capital may miscalculate.
**Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX includes numerical calculation questions on promoters' contribution. Understanding the post-issue capital base is essential for accurate computation.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In practice, the merchant banker must compute this well in advance of the issue to ensure the promoter has time to arrange the additional contribution and deposit it at least 1 day before the issue opens.
